mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 1.0% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows month-over-month growth exceeding 0.3%, the Fed is in a real-rate negative regime. Equity investors perceive this as a liquidity-driven environment that supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Real rates negative when nominal rates below inflation growth
failure_condition: In triggered periods, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 5.0% signals tight labor market conditions and wage pressure, which equity investors interpret as evidence of robust demand and earnings growth. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings expectations rise.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.9% as of decision date
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns when UNRATE < 5.0% are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 0.2% month-over-month while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1,100 thousand units, signaling weakening consumer and residential demand. This dual demand contraction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward earnings expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth < 0.2% AND HOUST < 1100 thousand units
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1099.0 thousand units as of decision date
failure_condition: In triggered periods, S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in fewer than 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month by more than 0.5%, indicating a supply-side contraction. This signals reduced capacity utilization and potential earnings pressure across manufacturing-heavy equity sectors, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 106.8477 as of decision date
failure_condition: In triggered periods, S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 52% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth accelerates above 500 thousand persons month-over-month while unemployment rate (UNRATE) simultaneously falls, creating a virtuous labor market cycle. This dual signal of accelerating job creation and tightening labor markets produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors price in sustained earnings growth.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 500 thousand AND UNRATE declines > 0.1pp
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 143560.0 thousand as of decision date
failure_condition: In triggered periods, 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) rises above 1.5% in a single month, signaling an unexpected monetary tightening shock. Equity investors interpret this as a threat to valuation multiples and near-term earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as risk-off sentiment dominates.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.38% as of decision date
failure_condition: In triggered periods, S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2.0% annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) simultaneously grow above 0.5% month-over-month, indicating broad-based demand expansion. This dual demand signal produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings growth expectations rise across sectors.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth > 2.0% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16455.1 billion as of decision date
failure_condition: In triggered periods, 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 0.4% month-over-month while nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) simultaneously grows above 400 thousand persons, signaling synchronized supply and labor expansion. This dual supply-side strength produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as capacity utilization and productivity expectations improve.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.4% AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 400 thousand
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 106.8477 and PAYEMS at 143560.0 as of decision date
failure_condition: In triggered periods, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 58% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
